serverInfo.name: sap.mcp/enterprise_information_management
serverInfo.title: enterprise_information_management
serverInfo.description: MCP server for the enterprise_information_management service.

tools.name: GET_tasks
tools.title: GET_tasks
tools.description: Returns a list of available tasks in the catalog of the SAP HANA system to which you are connected. SAP Web IDE package and flowgraph names are used to create task names. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Return the number of available tasks instead of a list of available tasks.

tools.name: GET_tasks_taskName
tools.title: GET_tasks_taskName
tools.description: Returns the metadata of a specific task.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task for which you want detailed metadata. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

tools.name: GET_tasks_taskName_tableTypes
tools.title: GET_tasks_taskName_tableTypes
tools.description: Returns table type information for a particular task.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task for which you want table type information. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Return the number of table types instead of a list of table types.

tools.name: GET_tasks_taskName_executions
tools.title: GET_tasks_taskName_executions
tools.description:
Retrieves all task execution data for a task that has been started programmatically.
For realtime tasks, returns the full details of the initial load and an aggregated summary for delta load tasks. For transactional tasks, returns only the aggregated summary of the task executions.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task for which you want execution data. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Return the number of executions for the specified task instead of the execution details.

parameters.name: summary
parameters.description: Return a summary of task execution information instead of execution details.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

tools.name: addtasks
tools.title: addtasks
tools.description: Programmatically starts execution of a particular task.

parameters.name: taskExecutionOptions
parameters.description:
Runtime parameters and data for the execution of a task. Information such as record data for a transactional task, substitution parameters, and table type parameters.
If executing a task that requires providing task data, table types, or parameter values via the request body, these values must be sent in JSON format and the request header content-type needs to be set to 'application/json'.
For example: `content-type = "application/json"`

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task you want to start. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

tools.name: PUT_tasks_taskName_executions
tools.title: PUT_tasks_taskName_executions
tools.description: Cancels the execution of a realtime flowgraph. This method works for realtime flowgraphs only because batch uses an execution ID. Canceling a realtime flow graph also resets the remote subscription.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task to cancel. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: waitTime
parameters.description: Time in seconds to wait for task to cancel before returning.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas."

tools.name: DELETE_tasks_taskName_executions
tools.title: DELETE_tasks_taskName_executions
tools.description: Deletes all task execution data for the specified task.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task for which to delete execution data. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: retention
parameters.description: Retention period for task execution data, in seconds. All data that is older than this value is deleted.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

tools.name: GET_tasks_taskName_executions_execId
tools.title: GET_tasks_taskName_executions_execId
tools.description: Returns task execution details for a single distinct execution of the specified task.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`

parameters.name: execId
parameters.description: ID of the specific task execution. The task execution ID is returned as `taskExecutionId` in the GET/tasks/{taskName}/executions response.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

tools.name: PUT_tasks_taskName_executions_execId
tools.title: PUT_tasks_taskName_executions_execId
tools.description: Stops the execution of a single distinct execution of the specified task.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`

parameters.name: execId
parameters.description: ID of the specific task execution. The task execution ID is returned as `taskExecutionId` in the GET/tasks/{taskName}/executions response.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task you want to stop. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: execId
parameters.description: ID of the specific task execution you want to stop. The task execution ID is returned as taskExecutionId in the GET/tasks/{taskName}/executions response.

parameters.name: waitTime
parameters.description: Time in seconds to wait for task to cancel before returning.

tools.name: DELETE_tasks_taskName_executions_execId
tools.title: DELETE_tasks_taskName_executions_execId
tools.description: Deletes the task execution details for a single distinct execution of the specified task.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`

parameters.name: execId
parameters.description: ID of the specific task execution. The task execution ID is returned as `taskExecutionId` in the GET/tasks/{taskName}/executions response.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

tools.name: GET_tasks_taskName_executions_execId_partitions
tools.title: GET_tasks_taskName_executions_execId_partitions
tools.description: Returns partition information for a single distinct execution of the specified task.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task for which you want partition information. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: execId
parameters.description: ID of the specific task execution for which you want partition information. The task execution ID is returned as taskExecutionId in the GET/tasks/{taskName}/executions response.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

tools.name: GET_tasks_taskName_executions_execId_operations
tools.title: GET_tasks_taskName_executions_execId_operations
tools.description: Returns operation-level details for a single distinct execution of the specified task.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task for which you want operation-level details. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: execId
parameters.description: ID of the specific task execution for which you want operation-level details. The task execution ID is returned as taskExecutionId in the GET/tasks/{taskName}/executions response.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

tools.name: GET_tasks_taskName_executions_execId_messages
tools.title: GET_tasks_taskName_executions_execId_messages
tools.description: Returns runtime messages associated with a single distinct execution of the specified task.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: Name of the task for which you want runtime messages. The task name must be specified in the same form it is returned in the GET/tasks response. For example, `<package>::<flowgraph>`.

parameters.name: execId
parameters.description: ID of the specific task execution for which you want runtime messages. The task execution ID is returned as taskExecutionId in the GET/tasks/{taskName}/executions response.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Severity of the messages to return. If severity is not specified, returns all runtime messages for the task execution.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Return the number of messages for the specified task execution instead of the message details.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the task. Required for catalog tasks that are not created through a flowgraph, where tasks with the same name exist in multiple schemas.

tools.name: GET_virtualTables
tools.title: GET_virtualTables
tools.description: Returns a list of all registered virtual tables.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Return the number of available virtual tables instead of a list of virtual tables.

tools.name: GET_virtualTables_table
tools.title: GET_virtualTables_table
tools.description:
Returns details allowing you to further identify a particular virtual table.
For example, if there is a virtual table named CONTACTS in schema A and a virtual table named CONTACTS in schema B, then you will need to specify the schema in order to correctly identify the correct table named CONTACTS for which you are looking.

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Name of the virtual table.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the table. Required if the virtual table name exists in more than one schema.

tools.name: POST_virtualTables_table
tools.title: POST_virtualTables_table
tools.description: Creates a new virtual table with the specified name in the catalog.

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Name of the virtual table.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the table. Required if the virtual table name exists in more than one schema.

tools.name: DELETE_virtualTables_table
tools.title: DELETE_virtualTables_table
tools.description:
Deletes a virtual table from the catalog.
`CASCADE` drops the virtual table and dependent objects while `RESTRICT` will drop the virtual table only when dependent objects do not exist.

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Name of the virtual table.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the table. Required if the virtual table name exists in more than one schema.

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Name of the virtual table to delete.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the table. Required if the virtual table name exists in more than one schema.

parameters.name: dropOptions
parameters.description: Table drop options.

tools.name: GET_virtualTables_table_properties
tools.title: GET_virtualTables_table_properties
tools.description: Retrieves the property information for a specific virtual table in the catalog.

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Name of the virtual table for which you want to return property information.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the table. Required if the virtual table name exists in more than one schema.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: If present, returns column properties for the specified column name.

tools.name: PUT_virtualTables_table_properties
tools.title: PUT_virtualTables_table_properties
tools.description: Modifies the properties of an existing virtual table in the catalog.

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Name of the virtual table that you want to modify.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Name of the catalog schema that contains the table. Required if the virtual table name exists in more than one schema.

parameters.name: set
parameters.description: Specifies whether to set or unset a property.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: When used in conjunction with the set parameter, it lets you set and unset virtual table column properties.
